title: thymus algeriensis var. abylaeus
intro: Thymus algeriensis var. abylaeus, also known as Abyl's thyme, is a subspecies of thyme native to the Atlas Mountains in Algeria. This perennial herb belongs to the mint family and is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it a popular choice in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Thymus algeriensis var. abylaeus include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting respiratory health, making it a useful treatment for coughs, colds, and flu. Herbal preparations of Abyl's thyme include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
